titleStmt.title: SII 27.59: original edition by M. D. Sampath, H. K. Narasimhamurthy, Madhav N. Katti and B. R. Gopal
No. 59. (A.R. No. 547 of 1929-30.) PADUVARI, COONDAPOOR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.

summary.p: Slab set up in a field by the side of the road leading to Sōmēśvara temple.

This damaged record registers a gift of land to Vaijanāthadēvaayyaṁgaḷu by eight individuals (praje) including two women, and the nāḍa-sēnabōva, of Bayidūru, when mahāpradhāna Bharamarasa-oḍeya was governing Bārakūru-rājya.

It is dated Śaka 1402, Kāḷayukta, Pushya śu. 10, Wednesday. The Śaka year is obviously wrong, the cyclic year Kāḷayukta, falling in the reign period of Harihara, corresponding to Śaka 1300 when the other details regularly correspond to 1378 A.D., December 29.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śrī gaṇādhipatayē namaḥ . namastuṁ bi caṁdracāmara cāravē trailōkya
nagarāraṁbha mūlastaṁbhāya śaṁ svasti śrī jayābhyudaya śakavaruṣa 1402
kāḷayukta saṁvatsarada puṣya śu 10 bu śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rāja paramēśvara śrī vīra
harihararāyana rājyābhyudayadalu Avara nirūpadiṁ śrīmanu mahāpradhānaṁ bharamarasa vo
ḍeyaru bārakūra rājyavanāḷu kāladalu śrīmatu vidyānātha dēvaṁgaḷige baidū
ranāḍa bīraṁṇaseṭṭiya maṁmagaḷu kaṁcuka ṟau nāyakana maṁmagaḷu kammā
ra Uḍuvara baḷiya kaṁcuka tuḷuvasēnabōvaku maṁjarana Aḷiya kōṭiseṭṭi da
gōviṁdaseṭṭi baṁkisēnabōva beṁmugaḍe Aṁtu I yō prajeU Ānāḍa sēna
bōva keṁgu sēnabōvanu taṁmūḷu Ēkastarāgi koṭa śilā śāsanakrama veṁ
teṁdare Ā vidyānātha dēvaṁgaḷige Uḍuvara baḷiya kaṁcukanu baidūra nāḍo
ḷage taṁna hiriyaru biṭamūlada Oḷage kaḷi hosa māṭavāgi
māḍidabāḷu kalakeṟiya keḷagaṇa kaga tu sīmeya vivara gōpa be
Aṟiyiṁdalū paḍuvalu teṁkalu ṁdalū baḍigalu
paḍuvalu tuṟubina nāgapaṁge tuḷuva sēnabōva bāḷiṁdalū mū
ḍalu baḍagalu muṭōḍiya hāruvaru daragade Iṁ
catusīmeya Oḷaguḷa bāḷige teṟuva sidāya Akaṁ Aṁnyā
gaḍi sebidaṁ
Ā vidyānā
damaged
Digital edition of SII 27.59 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
